pairs.question: What was the purpose of Children in Need 2010 in the UK?
pairs.answer: Children in Need 2010 was a fundraising campaign in the United Kingdom aimed at raising money for children in need. It was the 30th anniversary of the appeal and included a live broadcast on BBC One.

pairs.question: Who hosted the Children in Need 2010 broadcast and during what time slots?
pairs.answer: The broadcast was hosted by Terry Wogan throughout the event. Tess Daly hosted from 7 pm to 10 pm, Fearne Cotton from 10:35 pm to 2 am, and Peter Andre hosted from the BT Tower.

pairs.question: Which cities hosted regional opt-outs for the Children in Need 2010 broadcast?
pairs.answer: Regional opt-outs for the Children in Need 2010 broadcast were hosted from Cardiff, Glasgow, and Northern Ireland. Northern Ireland viewers saw the live broadcast from London for the first time instead of their own opt-out.

pairs.question: How did the fundraising total for Children in Need 2010 compare to 2009?
pairs.answer: The fundraising total for Children in Need 2010 was £18,098,199, which fell short of the £20,309,747 raised in 2009.

pairs.question: What was the change in the Northern Ireland broadcast format for Children in Need 2010 compared to previous years?
pairs.answer: In 2010, Northern Ireland viewers saw the live broadcast from London for the first time, instead of having their own opt-out as in previous years. The Northern Ireland concert was broadcast live on BBC Radio Ulster and highlights were shown on BBC One Northern Ireland the next day, hosted by Reggie Yates and John Daly.

pairs.question: Where was the main broadcast of Children in Need 2010 produced from?
pairs.answer: The main broadcast of Children in Need 2010 was produced from BBC Television Centre in London.

pairs.question: Who hosted the Northern Ireland content for Children in Need 2010 and how was it presented?
pairs.answer: The Northern Ireland content for Children in Need 2010 was hosted by Reggie Yates and John Daly. The concert was broadcast live on BBC Radio Ulster, and highlights were shown on BBC One Northern Ireland the next day.
